title: ranunculus acris subsp. atlanticus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. atlanticus, also known as the Atlantic meadow buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ranunculus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris subsp. atlanticus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it useful in treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
